preamble: The transformation from the uvular nasal /ɴ/ to the bilabial nasal [m] is a result of place assimilation. When the moraic nasal is followed by a bilabial sound, the point of articulation shifts to match the following consonant.

examples.note: Moraic nasal (ん) before labials like /p, b, m/.

sources: Vance, T. J. (2008). The Sounds of Japanese.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
